query: I track models in a Markdown note with columns: HF ID, quant, size, status (☁️=not downloaded, ✅=local). Parse the rows, check each ☁️ model against my HF caches at ~/.cache/huggingface and /Volumes/Storage/hf, filter those under 45 GB, skip 5-bit/6-bit when the same family already has a 4-bit and an 8-bit variant, sort by size, and give me a size-sorted table plus a ready-to-paste shell loop that downloads them into HF_HOME=/Volumes/Storage/hf.

I want to set up a JSON config that permanently tracks my two Hugging Face cache directories — main at ~/.cache/huggingface and an external drive at /Volumes/Storage/hf — so I can reference them by name in workflows. Temporary dry-run caches should not go in this file. Show me the minimal schema, validate it, and explain what each field does.

I need to audit my full HF model inventory across two caches: ~/.cache/huggingface and /Volumes/Storage/hf. For each cached repo, tell me whether it is fully downloaded and verified, metadata-only, or has missing shards/tokenizer files. Flag any that would fail hf cache verify --fail-on-missing-files. Summarize the overall disk usage per cache and list repos that appear in one cache but not the other.

I have a Markdown table of HF model IDs like mlx-community/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit in backtick code spans. I want every ID turned into a clickable Markdown link to https://huggingface.co/<repo-id> while keeping the table renderable in Obsidian (no footnote-style references, inline links only). Apply this to all rows and validate the result.

The Nemotron-3-Nano-30B model card has a comparison table showing GPT-OSS-20B column values for MMLU-Pro, AIME25, GPQA, SWE-Bench, TauBench, and RULER. The openai/gpt-oss-20b page itself shows MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond, SWE-bench Verified, HLE, and LEXam scores. Build a unified comparison table with both sources, mark which values came from the Nemotron page vs the GPT-OSS page, keep exact benchmark names without normalization, and note any score discrepancies between the two sources.

I want to get the exact on-disk size of mlx-community/Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct-4bit without touching my real HF caches. Use hf download --dry-run with a throwaway temp directory, parse the reported total, and tell me: (a) the full repo size, (b) whether it fits in a 45 GB remaining-space budget, and (c) the recommended shell command to actually download it to /Volumes/Storage/hf afterwards.

I need a shell loop that downloads a list of mlx-community and lmstudio-community repos into HF_HOME=/Volumes/Storage/hf. Quote every repo ID correctly, set HF_HUB_ETAG_TIMEOUT=60 and HF_HUB_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT=600, and make the result copy-pasteable as a single block.

My HF cache on /Volumes/Storage/hf is getting full. List the largest cached repos using hf cache ls with size filter and sort, identify detached revisions I can prune, and give me the rm/prune commands to reclaim at least 20 GB — with a dry-run preview first so I don't accidentally delete anything.
Fine-tune this Llama model with LoRA on my support dataset and give me the training script.
Explain what GPQA Diamond measures and whether it is a good benchmark for reasoning models.
Install transformers, accelerate, and bitsandbytes into my Python virtualenv.
Summarize this PDF and extract the tables into CSV.
Deploy a Text Generation Inference server for Mistral on Kubernetes.
I need a PowerPoint deck comparing AI coding tools for our leadership team.
Debug why this React table component rerenders too often when I sort columns.
Download this YouTube transcript and summarize it.
